$note: Captured snapshot of the COMPARED forecast/intelligence product for the azimuth benchmark (synthesis/benchmark.py). Quoted by product + capture date; NOT surfaced as an azimuth channel and NOT an L1 source. Refresh with pull_benchmark_foils.py.

foil.title: Grain/Energy price impact from Kerch Strait disruption
foil.scenario_short: Kerch Strait risk: critical. For now, the base case stays near 51% over the 30d. Actors absorb the current evidence mix without a decisive break toward either shock or relief.
foil.perspective_strategic: Kerch Strait risk: critical is setting the strategic baseline, and the current 47% probability implies a live but not settled risk path.
foil.perspective_contrarian: The current 58% confidence level still leaves room for reversal. If that restraint persists, the forecast can move below the current 47% baseline. Actors prioritize containment and the system drifts toward stabilization unless new hard signals emerge.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Black Sea becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 47%.

foil.title: Sovereign risk repricing from Black Sea maritime disruption state (supply_chain)
foil.scenario_short: Black Sea maritime disruption state (supply_chain), with 77% pressure and 74% state confirmation, keeps sovereign risk elevated through risk off rotation, as 7 urgent critical signals reinforce the path toward...
foil.perspective_strategic: The 77% pressure and 74% state confirmation signals indicate that Black Sea maritime disruption is structurally elevating sovereign risk through risk off rotation, with 1 clustered situation and 1 linked forecast reinforcing the transmission path.
foil.perspective_contrarian: The absence of directly matched reporting on Black Sea, as noted in triggers, could prevent escalation, while the 75% confidence level leaves room for reversal if no new hard signals emerge.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Black Sea becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 64%.
foil.top_signal: Black Sea maritime disruption state (supply_chain) is keeping sovereign risk elevated through risk off rotation, with 77% pressure and 74% state confirmation

seismic-infrastructure.absence_reason: No product publishes a seismic forecast — earthquakes are not predictable, so there is no forecast/intel alternative to azimuth's observed USGS record. This is the honest inverse: for an observed natural-hazard fact, the sourced record IS the only trustworthy source.
